author: mark
text:
Cross-platform diag pipe: pipe2 on Linux, pipe+fcntl elsewhere

libc::pipe2 is Linux-only, so 0.3.2 failed to compile on macOS.
Split make_pipe by cfg(target_os): Linux keeps the atomic pipe2
path; macOS/BSD fall back to pipe() + fcntl(FD_CLOEXEC) on each
endpoint. The classical pipe-then-fcntl race isn't reachable in
balls because prepare_diag_pipe is only called from the
synchronous plugin-invocation path.

Also wires a CI cross-check job that runs cargo check against
x86_64-apple-darwin on every push/PR so future Linux-only
syscalls landing in portable code are caught before merge instead
of by the next macOS user.

Linux coverage stays at 100%. Verified locally with
cargo check --target x86_64-apple-darwin --all-targets.
